title: Academic Achievement: Grading/Progress Reports to Parents
description: Conroe ISD's grading and progress reporting policy sets the rules for how teachers assign grades and how the district communicates student academic performance to families. It covers expectations around grading practices, the frequency of progress reports, and how parents can stay informed about their child's standing in each class. Understanding this policy helps families know what to expect when tracking their child's academic progress throughout the school year.

timeline_local.summary: This update to Conroe ISD's EIA (Grading/Progress Reports to Parents) policy makes several legislative-driven changes: it adds a new Definitions section clarifying "parent," allows electronic delivery and signing of written notices, replaces the generic nine-week report card language with a more specific nine-week written notice requirement including parental signature, replaces the vague interim report language with a detailed statutory requirement for unsatisfactory performance notices in foundation curriculum subjects, expands the Conferences section to require at least two in-person parent-teacher conferences per year, removes the reference to "conferences scheduled on the campus calendar," and adds a requirement for the District to adopt implementing procedures.
changes_added: Definitions section header and text: 'In this policy, "parent" includes a guardian, conservator, or other person having lawful control of a student.'

Progress Reporting introductory paragraph: 'In this Section, the District may provide written notices to parent's electronically and may permit a parent to sign written notices electronically through an electronic platform, provided the District retains a record verifying the parent's acknowledgment and offers the option of a handwritten signature.'

Notices exception paragraph: 'The notices under this Section shall not apply to a student who is 18 years of age or older and who is living in a different residence than the student's parents, is married, or has had the disabilities of minority removed for general purposes.'

Nine-Week Reports section: 'The District shall, at least once every nine weeks, provide written notice to a parent of a student's performance in each class or subject and require the parent's signature on the notice, to be returned to the District.'
Performance measurement sentence: 'Performance shall be measured in accordance with this Ppolicy, District procedures, and the standards established in EIE.'

Interim Reports replacement paragraph: 'Interim progress reports may be issued at the teacher's discretion; however, the District at least once every three weeks, or during the fourth week of each nine-week grading period, will give written notice to a parent or legal guardian of a student's performance in a subject included in the foundation curriculum under Section 28.002(a)(1) if the student's performance in the subject is consistently unsatisfactory, as determined by the District. The District may provide notice for other subjects. The notice required under this section shall provide for the signature of the student's parent and be returned to the District.'

Conferences section: 'The District shall provide for at least two opportunities for in-person conferences during each school year between each parent of a child enrolled in the District and the child's teachers.'
Closing sentence: 'The District shall adopt procedures to implement this Policy.'
changes_removed: The District shall issue grade reports/report cards every nine weeks on a form approved by the Superintendent or designee.

Interim Reports original paragraph: 'Interim progress reports may be issued at the teacher's discretion; however, notice of a student's consistent unsatisfactory performance shall be issued in accordance with law.'
In the Conferences section: 'to conferences scheduled on the campus calendar,'
timeline_local.notes: The cover page title heading reads 'Academic Achievement: Grading/Progress Reports to Parents (EIA)' without specifying a reading stage (e.g., 'first reading,' 'adopt,' etc.), so the action could not be determined from the title alone; marked as 'unknown.' The rational states 'Legislative changes.' Red underlined text and red strikethrough text were clearly distinguishable throughout the document. The 'Ppolicy' capitalization anomaly in the Performance sentence appears to be a drafting artifact in the redline.

timeline_local.summary: This update to Conroe ISD's EIA (Local) policy adds three new requirements: (1) parents must sign and return grade reports every 12 weeks, (2) interim progress reports for consistently unsatisfactory performance must also require a parent signature and be returned, and (3) parents must be provided at least two in-person conference opportunities per school year with their child's teacher. All signature requirements include an exemption for students 18 or older living independently, married, or with disabilities of minority removed.
changes_added: The District shall require a parent's signature on the report, to be returned to the District every 12 weeks. This shall not apply to a student who is 18 years of age or older and who is living in a different residence than the student's parents, is married, or has had the disabilities of minority removed for general purposes.

If a student's performance in the subject is consistently unsatisfactory, as determined by EIE(Local), the student's interim progress report shall require a parent's signature and be returned to the District. This shall not apply to a student who is 18 years of age or older and who is living in a different residence than the student's parents, is married, or has had the disabilities of minority removed for general purposes.
A student's parent shall be provided with at least two opportunities for in-person conferences during each school year between themselves and their child's teacher.
timeline_local.notes: The cover page does not contain a standard TASB action title heading (e.g., "Discuss Revisions to..." or "Adopt Revisions to..."). The document is labeled Version 1 with no date issued, and the cover page title simply reads "Academic Achievement: Grading/Progress Reports to Parents (EIA)" without a procedural action phrase. Action has been set to 'unknown' as a result. All three red-underlined additions were clearly identifiable. No red strikethrough (removed) text was present in the document.

description_local: This policy covers updates to five different local school district policies, all revised in response to recent changes in Texas law, addressing topics ranging from employee legal protections and student grading practices to medical treatment, crisis intervention, and child abuse reporting. The changes affect a broad range of people connected to the school community, including teachers and other staff, students, and parents. Parents and community members should be aware of these updates because they directly impact how schools handle student grades and progress reporting, respond to health and safety emergencies, protect children from abuse and neglect, and support the legal rights of school employees.
